mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative quarterly returns in >60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term loses predictive power (IC < 0.01) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 50% and a negative cumulative alpha over a 24-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in retail sales indicates an abrupt collapse in consumer demand, forcing immediate equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in >65% of periods following a retail sales drop exceeding the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative quarterly returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, increasing equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of periods where inflation remains above the threshold during tightening cycles, or the signal information coefficient turns negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal rising construction activity and broader economic confidence, which correlates with immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Housing starts data fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a directional accuracy below 45% and a negative cumulative return in a 36-month out-of-sample backtest.
